title: Optimality certificate delivered after the dispatch deadline
prompt: Routes must be released at 08:00. An exact solver finds a feasible incumbent before 08:00 but closes the optimality gap and issues a certificate at 08:30, after vehicles have departed. A suggested story says the certificate improves the executed morning routes. Diagnose the timing failure while distinguishing pre-deadline solution quality from post-deadline proof.

model_specification.mathematical_objective: minimize monetary routing and lateness cost
model_specification.mechanism: Branch-and-bound improves feasible incumbents and bounds over time; gap closure at 08:30 certifies the model optimum only after the 08:00 execution decision.

fidelity_requirements: Only the incumbent available by 08:00 can affect executed routes
The 08:30 certificate validates model optimality but cannot retroactively improve the released plan
prohibited_inferences: The post-deadline certificate changes the executed morning routes

evidence_needs.claim: Certificates are routinely available before the release deadline
